evals.description: Skill generates a well-structured storyboard for a standard business presentation
evals.prompt: Create a storyboard for a 20-minute presentation on our company's 2026 product roadmap. Audience: product managers and engineers. Key messages: we are pivoting to AI, we ship faster than competitors, Q3 is our biggest release.
assertions: The storyboard has approximately 10 slides (20 min ÷ 2 min/slide)
Each slide has: title, message, objective, duration, visual suggestion, transition note
All three key messages appear as slide headlines
The 3-act structure is present: Context, Content, Action sections
No slide carries more than one concept

evals.description: Skill limits slide count for very short presentations (under 5 min)
evals.prompt: Storyboard a 4-minute lightning talk about our new onboarding flow. Audience: internal team.
assertions: The storyboard has at most 4 slides
The structure is: title / problem / solution / call-to-action
Each slide has a duration ≤ 1.5 min
The skill does not pad with unnecessary slides

evals.description: Skill suggests appropriate visual types that match the data or content
evals.prompt: Build a storyboard for a 30-minute data-driven presentation on customer churn analysis. Audience: C-suite.
assertions: Slides with quantitative data suggest charts (bar, line, pie) not text-only
Slides with process flows suggest diagrams or flowcharts
Slides with executive recommendations use minimal bullets or a single bold statement
No visual suggestion contradicts the slide content type

evals.description: Skill requests missing required inputs before generating
evals.prompt: Create a storyboard about digital transformation.
assertions: The skill detects that audience and duration are missing
The skill uses vscode_askQuestions or asks clearly for the missing fields
The skill does NOT generate a storyboard before collecting audience and duration
The skill does not ask for more than 3-4 items at once

evals.description: Skill warns and suggests splitting when duration exceeds 45 minutes
evals.prompt: Create a storyboard for a 60-minute all-hands presentation about company strategy.
assertions: The skill warns that 60 minutes is over the recommended limit
The skill recommends splitting into two 30-minute modules
If the user still wants a single deck, the skill proceeds and notes the risk of audience fatigue
The storyboard, if generated, includes natural breakpoints at the halfway mark
